name: Eid al-Fitr
traditions: Communal prayers at mosques; charitable giving (Zakat al-Fitr); festive meals with family; new clothing; visiting relatives and friends; children receive gifts and money.
historicalBackground: Eid al-Fitr marks the end of Ramadan, the holy month of fasting. Established by Prophet Muhammad in Medina, it celebrates spiritual renewal and gratitude.
description: The Festival of Breaking the Fast, one of the two major Islamic holidays celebrated by Muslims worldwide after the holy month of Ramadan.
